solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public boolean isBoomerang(int[][] points) {
        // Check if all three points are distinct
        if (points[0][0] == points[1][0] && points[0][1] == points[1][1]) return false;
        if (points[1][0] == points[2][0] && points[1][1] == points[2][1]) return false;
        if (points[0][0] == points[2][0] && points[0][1] == points[2][1]) return false;

        // Check if the three points are not collinear using area of triangle formula
        int area = (points[1][0] - points[0][0]) * (points[2][1] - points[0][1]) 
                 - (points[2][0] - points[0][0]) * (points[1][1] - points[0][1]);
        return area != 0;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution checks if all three points are distinct and then uses the area of a triangle formed by the three points to determine if they are collinear. If the area is zero, the points are collinear. This approach is efficient with constant time and space complexity.>
